name: Chupacabra
description: The Chupacabra, meaning 'goat-sucker', is a legendary cryptid rumored to inhabit parts of Latin America. Its first reported attacks were in Puerto Rico in 1995, and sightings have since spread throughout the Americas, including Mexico, the United States, and even as far south as Chile. Descriptions of the Chupacabra vary widely, fueling debate about its true nature. Some describe it as a reptilian creature with leathery or scaly skin, while others depict it as a canine-like beast resembling a hairless dog or coyote. Regardless of the specific description, the Chupacabra is consistently associated with the unexplained deaths of livestock, particularly goats, which are found drained of blood with puncture wounds to the neck. This has led to widespread fear and speculation, with many attributing the attacks to supernatural forces or extraterrestrial beings. While no definitive proof of the Chupacabra's existence has ever been found, the enduring legend continues to capture the imagination and terrify farmers throughout the Americas. The creature's elusiveness and the gruesome nature of its alleged attacks have solidified its place in modern folklore, blending elements of fear, mystery, and the unknown.
subtitle: The Goat-Sucking Cryptid of the Americas
appearance: Descriptions vary. Some describe a reptilian creature with leathery skin, about 3-4 feet tall, with spines running down its back and glowing red eyes. Others describe a hairless, canine-like creature, similar in size to a coyote, with sharp teeth and powerful claws. Some accounts mention a strong, musky odor.
habitat: Rural areas, forests, and farms throughout Latin America and the Southern United States. Often near livestock.
behavior: Primarily nocturnal and solitary. Highly territorial and aggressive when threatened. Allegedly hunts livestock, particularly goats, draining them of their blood through puncture wounds to the neck. Elusive and difficult to track.

keyMyths.title: The Puerto Rican Outbreak
keyMyths.description: The first widespread reports of Chupacabra attacks originated in Puerto Rico in 1995, with numerous livestock deaths attributed to an unknown creature. These initial reports fueled the legend and sparked similar sightings throughout the Americas.
keyMyths.source: Various news reports and eyewitness accounts from Puerto Rico in 1995.

sources.author: Benjamin Radford
sources.work: Tracking the Chupacabra: The Vampire Beast in Fact, Fiction, and Folklore
sources.description: A skeptical investigation into the Chupacabra phenomenon, exploring its origins and evolution.
sources.text: A skeptical investigation into the Chupacabra phenomenon, exploring its origins and evolution.
